Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Jul-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Motion for Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds.
Dept. 514, Judge: Kathleen Kelly; Clerk: J. Bertelsen. Proceedings reported by: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.
Geoff T. Sloniker, Esq. appeared for Donald Landry as Successor-in-Interest to and Wrongful Death Heir of Marcelle Landry, deceased and as Successor-in-Interest to Kathleen Landry as Wrongful Death Heir of Marcelle Landry, deceased; and Marc Landry and Kathleen Morris as Wrongful Death Heirs of Marcelle Landry, Deceased.
Witnesses Donald Landry and Lissa Landry, were present, were sworn and testified.
No other appearances. Marc Landry and Kathleen Morris submitted declarations.
After hearing the testimony and considered the evidence both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, the Court ordered for distribution of all fund (both litigation funds and funds from claims against bankrupt asbestos defendant trusts) negotiated after the December 1, 2015 death of Marcelle Landry in the following proportions:
Estate of MARCELL LANDRY 0%
Estate of Kathleen Landry as Wrongful Death Heir 0%
Marc Landry as Wrongful Death Heir 30%
Donald Landry as Wrongful Death Heir 40%
Kathleen Morris as Wrongful Death Heir 30%
The Signature of plaintiff Donald Landry alone is sufficient to bind the estate and wrongful death heirs on all settlement documents.
Order was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Dept. 514, Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Dustin Seesemann, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Kathleen Landry, as Successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Marcelle Landry, deceased; and Marc Landry, Donald Landry, Kathleen Morris, as wrongful death heirs of Marcelle Landry.

Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.

The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Oct-27-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Kathleen Landry as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Marcelle Landry, deceased, et al.
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

No objection made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference and to advance trial date is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The July 10, 2017 trial date is advanced to February 21, 2017 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is February 24, 2017. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015, now included in Local Rule 20, shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 9, 2017. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than January 6, 2017. Responses to the demand are due no later than January 20, 2017. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by February 14, 2017 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on February 16, 2017. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 3, 2017. Expert discovery cut-off date is February 17, 2017. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than December 9, 2016. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. (Mini Minutes continued, see next entry.)
